title: Floral Dense Canadian Onion Frittata
description: A Jain-friendly, dense, and flavorful Canadian-inspired frittata featuring yellow onions, perfect for a brunch mood. This dish combines the sweetness of caramelized onions with the freshness of floral notes.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the sliced onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are caramelized and golden brown, about 20-25 minutes.
In a small bowl, mix together the dried rose petals and lavender buds.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, salt, and black pepper.
Add the shredded cheese to the egg mixture and stir to combine.
Add the cooked onions, rose petal mixture, and chopped parsley to the egg mixture. Stir gently to combine.
Pour the egg mixture into a greased 9-inch (23cm) skillet or frittata pan.
Cook the frittata over medium heat, without stirring, until the edges start to set, about 5 minutes.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the frittata is cooked through and golden brown on top.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes. Slice the frittata into wedges and serve hot.
